title: PV Sindhu Reaches Japan Open Semifinal, Her First Super 750 Last Four
indian_angle: India's top female shuttler PV Sindhu has achieved a significant milestone by reaching her first Super 750 semifinal at the Japan Open.
summary: PV Sindhu advanced to the semifinals of the Japan Open, marking her first appearance in the last four of a Super 750 tournament, following the withdrawal of Nozomi Okuhara.

title: Delhi World Championships 2026: Why Badminton Needs Gen Z, Alpha Fans
indian_angle: With India set to host the BWF World Championships in Delhi in 2026, there's a focus on attracting younger generations to grow the sport's fan base domestically.
summary: An analysis highlights the importance of engaging Gen Z and Gen Alpha audiences for the success and future of badminton, especially with the upcoming World Championships in Delhi in 2026.

title: BAI President Sarma on Monkeys at India Open: 'What is the Problem?'
indian_angle: The President of the Badminton Association of India, Himanta Biswa Sarma, addressed the unusual presence of monkeys at the India Open, sparking discussion within the Indian badminton community.
summary: BAI President Himanta Biswa Sarma made a lighthearted comment regarding the presence of monkeys during the India Open, questioning why it would be considered a problem for spectators.

title: Badminton's New 3x15 Scoring System to Debut in Indian Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has implemented a new 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships, impacting junior age groups.
summary: The 3x15 scoring system, aimed at U-13, U-15, and U-17 age groups, will be introduced in all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships, starting with an event in Ernakulam.

title: Kidambi Srikanth Finishes as Runner-Up at US Open Super 300
indian_angle: Indian shuttler Kidambi Srikanth reached the final of the US Open, demonstrating strong form in a Super 300 event.
summary: Kidambi Srikanth advanced to the final of the US Open but was unable to secure the title, finishing as the runner-up after a commendable performance throughout the tournament.

title: BAI Launches Grassroot Coaches Development Program in Guwahati & Raipur
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India is conducting a Coaches Development Program at the grassroot level in Guwahati and Raipur, aiming to enhance coaching standards across the country.
summary: A new Coaches Development Program for grassroot level coaches is being held at National Centres of Excellence in Guwahati and Raipur from July 15th to 24th, 2026, to improve coaching quality.
